attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Examine a cell phone next to the light of the tall lamp.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The provided images depict a room with a warm, orange-yellow wall and a light brown parquet floor. The scene contains several pieces of furniture and objects, but none of them match the types defined in the given PDDL domain file (`microwave`, `fridge`, `sink`, `knife`, `receptacle`, or `agent`). The instruction to "Examine a cell phone next to the light of the tall lamp" cannot be fulfilled based on the domain, as there is no `cell phone` object type defined, nor any object that can be logically inferred to be a cell phone from the visual information or the domain predicates.

**Objects and Types (Domain-Relevant):**
- There are **no objects** in the scene that correspond to the types `microwave`, `fridge`, `sink`, `knife`, or `receptacle` as defined in the domain.
- The domain defines `receptacle` as an object that can hold things. While there are dark wooden furniture pieces (possibly nightstands or cabinets) and a large golden cylindrical object (possibly a trash can or decorative vase), none are explicitly identified as `receptacle` in the domain, and their function is not confirmed by the visual data.
- The tall lamp with a green textured shade and a light-colored wooden base is visible, but it is not a defined object type in the domain. It cannot be classified as a `receptacle` or any other type from the domain.

**Quantities:**
- **0** objects of type `microwave`.
- **0** objects of type `fridge`.
- **0** objects of type `sink`.
- **0** objects of type `knife`.
- **0** objects of type `receptacle` (as per domain definition).
- **0** objects of type `agent`.
- **0** objects that can be identified as a `cell phone`.

**Spatial Relationships:**
- The tall lamp is positioned against the wall, with its base on the parquet floor.
- The dark wooden furniture pieces are placed along the wall, with one appearing to be a nightstand or cabinet next to the lamp.
- The golden cylindrical object is on the floor, near the furniture.
- There is no spatial relationship defined in the domain predicates (e.g., `inReceptacle`, `atLocation`, `holds`) that can be applied, as no domain objects are present.

**Current State:**
- No objects have a defined state (e.g., `opened`, `isClean`, `isHot`, `isCool`, `isOn`, `isSliced`, `isToggled`) because no domain objects are present.
- The lamp is not toggled on or off in the images; its state is unknown and not defined in the domain.

**Visual Properties:**
- The wall is a solid, warm orange-yellow color.
- The floor is a light brown parquet pattern.
- The lamp has a green, textured, bell-shaped shade and a light-colored wooden base.
- The furniture is dark brown or black wood.
- The golden cylindrical object has a metallic sheen and is partially visible.

In summary, the scene does not contain any objects that match the types or predicates defined in the `put_task` domain. The instruction to examine a cell phone next to the lamp cannot be executed within this domain, as the required object (cell phone) does not exist in the domain, and no domain objects are present in the scene.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
The provided scene contains no objects that match the types defined in the `put_task` domain (`microwave`, `fridge`, `sink`, `knife`, `receptacle`, or `agent`). The instruction to “examine a cell phone next to the light of the tall lamp” cannot be represented because:
- There is no `cell phone` object type in the domain.
- The lamp and furniture are not defined as domain objects and cannot be classified as `receptacle` or any other type without explicit domain support.
- No domain predicates (e.g., `atLocation`, `holds`, `inReceptacle`) can be applied, as no domain objects exist in the scene.

Therefore, it is impossible to generate a valid PDDL problem that satisfies the domain constraints and the instruction. The domain lacks the necessary object types and predicates to model the scene or the task.

**Conclusion**: No PDDL problem can be generated for this scene and instruction under the given domain.

attempts.error:
Predicate not defined in domain:
  and

SCENE CAPTION:
The provided images do not contain any objects that match the types defined in the domain file (microwave, fridge, sink, knife, or any receptacle that can hold objects as specified). The scene consists of furniture and decorative items: a dark wood nightstand with drawers, a golden cylindrical trash can, a tall lamp with a green textured lampshade and a light-colored wooden base, and a parquet wood floor against a mustard-yellow wall. There is no cell phone visible in any of the images. Therefore, none of the domain predicates (e.g., `inReceptacle`, `holds`, `isClean`, `isHot`, `isCool`, `isOn`, `isToggled`, `isSliced`, `atLocation`) can be applied to any object in the scene as they relate to the defined types. The instruction to “examine a cell phone next to the light of the tall lamp” cannot be fulfilled based on the visible objects, as no cell phone is present and no object in the scene is of a type that can be manipulated according to the domain actions. The tall lamp is positioned near the nightstand and trash can, and its lampshade is illuminated, casting light on the surrounding area, but no relevant receptacles or manipulable objects from the domain are present.

Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
The provided scene does not contain any objects that match the types defined in the domain file (microwave, fridge, sink, knife, or any receptacle that can hold objects as specified). Furthermore, the instruction to “examine a cell phone next to the light of the tall lamp” cannot be fulfilled because no cell phone is present in the scene, and no object in the scene is of a type that can be manipulated according to the domain actions.

Since the domain only allows actions on specific types (receptacles, knife, etc.) and the scene contains none of these, and since the goal involves an object (cell phone) not present in the scene, it is impossible to construct a valid PDDL problem that satisfies the domain constraints and the instruction.

Therefore, no valid PDDL problem can be generated for this scenario under the given domain.

However, to strictly adhere to the output format requirement, we can generate a minimal PDDL problem that declares the agent and no other objects, with an empty goal, reflecting that no valid actions can be performed:

```pddl
(define (problem examine_cell_phone)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
    )
    (:init
        ; No objects of domain types are present, so no predicates can be asserted.
    )
    (:goal (and))
)
```

This problem is technically valid PDDL but reflects the impossibility of fulfilling the instruction within the domain’s constraints.

    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.

    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Examine a cell phone next to the light of the tall lamp.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
Scene Description:

The scene contains a room with a parquet wooden floor and a solid orange-yellow wall. A white baseboard runs along the bottom of the wall.

Relevant Objects and Types:

1. Tall Lamp:
   - Type: object (not a receptacle)
   - Description: A tall floor lamp with a light-colored, possibly beige or off-white, rectangular wooden base and a slender black pole. The lampshade is large, conical, and textured with a woven or grid-like pattern in a muted green or olive color. The lamp is positioned near the wall and is the primary source of illumination in the scene.
   - State: The lamp is currently on (isOn true), as evidenced by the bright glow and shadow cast on the floor and wall. It has been toggled (isToggled true).

2. Cell Phone:
   - Type: object (not a receptacle)
   - Description: A small, dark rectangular object, likely black or dark gray, lying on the wooden floor. It is positioned on the floor to the left of the tall lamp’s base, near the wall. It is not being held by any agent and is not inside any receptacle.
   - State: The cell phone is not currently held (holdsAny false for any agent), not cleaned, not heated, not cooled, and not sliced. It is resting on the floor.

3. Dark Wooden Side Table / Nightstand:
   - Type: receptacle (can hold objects)
   - Description: A dark brown or black rectangular piece of furniture with a flat top and at least one visible drawer. It is placed against the wall, to the right of the tall lamp in the first image. In the third image, it is seen from a different angle, confirming its position near the wall and adjacent to a golden cylindrical object.
   - State: It is closed (not opened). It is not currently being interacted with by an agent.

4. Golden Cylindrical Object (possibly a trash can or decorative vase):
   - Type: receptacle (can hold objects)
   - Description: A shiny, metallic gold-colored cylinder with a slightly flared rim. It is positioned on the floor to the left of the dark wooden side table and near the wall. In the first image, it is partially visible, and in the third image, it is seen from above, showing its open top and a greenish interior.
   - State: It is closed (not opened) and not currently being interacted with.

5. Agent (robot):
   - Type: agent
   - Description: The robot is not visible in the images but is assumed to be present and initially not at any object’s location (atLocation false for all objects).

Spatial Relationships:

- The tall lamp is positioned against the wall, with its base on the wooden floor.
- The cell phone is on the floor, to the left of the tall lamp’s base and near the wall.
- The dark wooden side table is against the wall, to the right of the tall lamp.
- The golden cylindrical object is on the floor, to the left of the dark wooden side table and near the wall, adjacent to the cell phone’s location.
- The lamp is the closest object to the cell phone, and the cell phone is positioned next to the light emitted by the lamp.

Relevant Predicates (Current State):

- (isOn tall_lamp) — true
- (isToggled tall_lamp) — true
- (atLocation robot ?) — false for all objects (robot is not yet at any location)
- (holds robot ?) — false (robot is not holding any object)
- (holdsAny robot) — false
- (inReceptacle cell_phone ?) — false (cell phone is on the floor, not in any receptacle)
- (opened side_table) — false
- (opened golden_cylinder) — false

(define (problem examine_cell_phone)

final.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
